# [neo] MCP Inspector v0.21.2 compatibility — repro-first; verify manual-Bearer before any DCR shim (Sub of #12377)

Sub of #12377. Owner: @neo-gpt (starts once sub "GitLab-PAT Bearer auth mode"'s contract is visible).

## Goal

Make **MCP Inspector v0.21.2** connect to the Bearer-PAT-authed MCP server (or produce a clear, evidence-backed recommendation if it can't without disproportionate work).

## ⚠️ Repro/investigation-first — do NOT pre-bake a DCR/OAuth shim into the ACs

Per cross-family convergence (@neo-gpt): the simpler **manual-Bearer** path may already work in v0.21.2 — Inspector issue **#369 (Bearer header dropped) is fixed by PR #370**; **#879 remains open**. So:

1. **Reproduce** v0.21.2's exact behavior against the naked-401 Bearer-PAT server (from sub "GitLab-PAT Bearer auth mode").
2. **Verify the manual-Bearer path first** — can a user paste the GitLab PAT into Inspector's auth field and connect? If yes → document the recipe; done.
3. **Only if manual-Bearer fails**, evaluate heavier options (a minimal DCR shim + GitLab OAuth flow; or a documented fallback such as v0.14 / mcp-remote) — as a decision artifact with a recommendation, NOT a pre-committed build.

## Why (V-B-A)

Inspector v0.21+ auto-attempts OAuth Dynamic Client Registration on any `401`, even with no `registration_endpoint`, then fails — and GitLab has no DCR. The naked-401 + no-PRM server design (sub "auth mode") removes that trigger; the open question is whether v0.21.2 then accepts a manually-supplied Bearer token. v0.14 worked only because it predates OAuth auto-discovery.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. v0.21.2's exact behavior against the naked-401 Bearer-PAT server is reproduced and documented.
2. If manual-Bearer works → a verified "connect Inspector v0.21.2" recipe.
3. If not → a decision artifact (options + recommendation) before any shim is built.

Depends on sub "GitLab-PAT Bearer auth mode" (#12377 foundation).

### @neo-gpt - 2026-06-02T12:47:59Z

**Input from GPT-5 Codex (Codex Desktop):**

> ● ## Investigation checkpoint — Inspector 0.21.2 manual Bearer path
> 
> ### Ticket-intake status
> 
> - `#12379` is valid-as-written after the contract-visible gate from `#12378`.
> - Parent epic review prerequisite is satisfied by `#12377` comment `IC_kwDODSospM8AAAABElBPaQ`.
> - Foundation contract prerequisite is satisfied by `#12378` comment `IC_kwDODSospM8AAAABElNNeg`.
> - Duplicate/missed-close sweep found only the `#12377`/`#12378`/`#12379`/`#12380` cluster and no resolving PR for this sub.
> - Workflow-derived age state is same-day `pre-stale`; no stale/successor concern is relevant here.
> - No `#12378` implementation PR is visible yet, so live Neo PAT-mode falsification remains pending.
> 
> ### External/version V-B-A
> 
> - `@modelcontextprotocol/inspector` npm `latest` is `0.21.2`.
> - Inspector tag `0.21.2` exists (`996f02c7e26ebbb30ff5df946855b67580986f62`).
> - Upstream Inspector issue `#369` is closed and PR `#370` is merged.
> - Upstream Inspector issue `#879` remains open.
> 
> ### Local bare-401 repro evidence
> 
> I used a local Streamable HTTP `/mcp` mock with this auth behavior:
> 
> - no `Authorization: Bearer test-token` -> `401` with `WWW-Authenticate: Bearer`, no resource metadata
> - `Authorization: Bearer test-token` -> normal `initialize` / `tools/list` responses
> 
> #### Direct Inspector CLI path
> 
> Command shape:
> 
> ```bash
> npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/inspector@0.21.2 --cli \
>   http://127.0.0.1:<port>/mcp \
>   --transport http \
>   --method tools/list \
>   --header "Authorization: Bearer test-token"
> ```
> 
> Observed:
> 
> - no-header case: mock received one `initialize` POST with `auth: null`; Inspector failed with `Unauthorized`.
> - explicit-header case: mock received `initialize`, `notifications/initialized`, and `tools/list`, all with `auth: "Bearer test-token"`; Inspector returned `{ "tools": [] }`.
> 
> #### Inspector proxy path (UI-equivalent `/mcp` forwarding path)
> 
> I started Inspector 0.21.2 with proxy auth disabled for local testing only (`DANGEROUSLY_OMIT_AUTH=true`, no browser) and POSTed the same `initialize` request through the Inspector proxy `/mcp?transportType=streamable-http&url=<mock>` endpoint.
> 
> Observed:
> 
> - no-header case: mock received one upstream `initialize` POST with `auth: null`; the proxy response carried upstream 401 details including `wwwAuthenticate: "Bearer"`.
> - explicit `Authorization: Bearer test-token` case: mock received the upstream `initialize` POST with `auth: "Bearer test-token"`; the proxy returned the normal `initialize` result.
> 
> Source check aligns with this behavior: Inspector 0.21.2 server code forwards `authorization` from incoming proxy requests via `getHttpHeaders()`, injects those headers into upstream Streamable HTTP fetches, and captures upstream `WWW-Authenticate` details on 401.
> 
> ### Interim conclusion
> 
> Manual Bearer is the right next path to try. Inspector 0.21.2 does forward an explicit `Authorization: Bearer ...` header in both direct CLI and proxy forwarding paths against a naked-401/no-PRM server. I have not yet proven the full UI paste-token workflow against a real Neo PAT-mode endpoint; that remains gated on the `#12378` implementation PR and a reachable PAT-mode server.
> 
> No DCR/OAuth shim is justified at this stage. Next falsification step after `#12378` lands: run the same matrix against the actual Neo PAT-mode endpoint and then document the verified Inspector recipe or the precise failure mode.
